summary: Postinstall credential stealer exfiltrates SSH keys,.env files, and secret env vars to hardcoded IP
details: The package's postinstall.js lifecycle script runs automatically on `npm install` and harvests installer secrets: it scans the current working directory, home directory, and ~/.ssh for private key material (id_rsa, id_ed25519, *.pem, *.key, *.cred), reads.env files, and iterates process.env for keys matching PRIVATE/SECRET/TOKEN/API_KEY/PASSWORD/MNEMONIC/SEED/WALLET/AWS. The collected file contents and environment variables are POSTed together with host identifiers (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd()) to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://107.161.90.180:7777 over plain HTTP. The package advertises itself as an 'Unofficial polymarket-sdk SDK' under the unrelated @wagni_bot scope, impersonating the Polymarket SDK namespace to lure installers, while shipping the credential stealer as the only functional code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
